content == {{神社 |名称= Okanoue Shrine |画像= [[File:Okanoue shrine, Tokushima.jpg|250px]]
Shimenawa pillar and torii |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}} Ōtera Aza Okayamaji 7 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former village shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Okanoue Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okanoue Shrine''' (おかのうえじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its year of foundation is unknown. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}} (Kansei 1), it moved to the current site. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here and prayed for lasting military fortune while heading from landing at Katsuura in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} to {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. There is also a theory that "Okanoue" was originally read "Okanokami," and is a corruption of "Uka no kami." Within the precincts stands a {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} called "Ōkusu of Okanomiya," designated a Tokushima Prefectural Natural Monument. It has long been called the "Sacred Mirror Tree." With a girth of about 25 m, its trunk splits from one root into three, and one of those splits again, so four trunks stand side by side as if pressing against each other. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} === Precinct shrine === * Jisha Shrine === Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Itano Interchange|Q11105197|WD=Q11105197|1=Itano interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Itano Station|Q8190673|WD=Q8190673|1=Itano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kōtoku Line|Q859984|WD=Q859984|1=Kōtoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/tokushima/364045/detail/36404ac2100120888 岡上神社の大クス 日本観光振興協会] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:板野町の建築物]] [[Category:板野町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

content == {{神社 |名称= Okanoue Shrine |画像= [[File:Okanoue shrine, Tokushima.jpg|250px]]
Shimenawa pillar and torii |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}} Ōtera Aza Okayamaji 7 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former village shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Okanoue Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okanoue Shrine''' (おかのうえじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its year of foundation is unknown. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}} (Kansei 1), it moved to the current site. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here and prayed for lasting military fortune while heading from landing at Katsuura in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} to {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. There is also a theory that "Okanoue" was originally read "Okanokami," and is a corruption of "Uka no kami." Within the precincts stands a {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} called "Ōkusu of Okanomiya," designated a Tokushima Prefectural Natural Monument. It has long been called the "Sacred Mirror Tree." With a girth of about 25 m, its trunk splits from one root into three, and one of those splits again, so four trunks stand side by side as if pressing against each other. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} === Precinct shrine === * Jisha Shrine === Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Itano Interchange|Q11105197|WD=Q11105197|1=Itano interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Itano Station|Q8190673|WD=Q8190673|1=Itano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kōtoku Line|Q859984|WD=Q859984|1=Kōtoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/tokushima/364045/detail/36404ac2100120888 岡上神社の大クス 日本観光振興協会] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:板野町の建築物]] [[Category:板野町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Shimenawa pillar and torii |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}} Ōtera Aza Okayamaji 7 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former village shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Okanoue Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okanoue Shrine''' (おかのうえじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its year of foundation is unknown. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}} (Kansei 1), it moved to the current site. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here and prayed for lasting military fortune while heading from landing at Katsuura in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} to {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. There is also a theory that "Okanoue" was originally read "Okanokami," and is a corruption of "Uka no kami." Within the precincts stands a {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} called "Ōkusu of Okanomiya," designated a Tokushima Prefectural Natural Monument. It has long been called the "Sacred Mirror Tree." With a girth of about 25 m, its trunk splits from one root into three, and one of those splits again, so four trunks stand side by side as if pressing against each other. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} === Precinct shrine === * Jisha Shrine === Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Itano Interchange|Q11105197|WD=Q11105197|1=Itano interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Itano Station|Q8190673|WD=Q8190673|1=Itano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kōtoku Line|Q859984|WD=Q859984|1=Kōtoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/tokushima/364045/detail/36404ac2100120888 岡上神社の大クス 日本観光振興協会] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:板野町の建築物]] [[Category:板野町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

There is also a theory that "Okanoue" was originally read "Okanokami," and is a corruption of "Uka no kami." Within the precincts stands a {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} called "Ōkusu of Okanomiya," designated a Tokushima Prefectural Natural Monument. It has long been called the "Sacred Mirror Tree." With a girth of about 25 m, its trunk splits from one root into three, and one of those splits again, so four trunks stand side by side as if pressing against each other. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} === Precinct shrine === * Jisha Shrine === Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Itano Interchange|Q11105197|WD=Q11105197|1=Itano interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Itano Station|Q8190673|WD=Q8190673|1=Itano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kōtoku Line|Q859984|WD=Q859984|1=Kōtoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. === content == {{神社 |名称= Okanoue Shrine |画像= [[File:Okanoue shrine, Tokushima.jpg|250px]]
Shimenawa pillar and torii |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}} Ōtera Aza Okayamaji 7 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former village shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Okanoue Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okanoue Shrine''' (おかのうえじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its year of foundation is unknown. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}} (Kansei 1), it moved to the current site. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here and prayed for lasting military fortune while heading from landing at Katsuura in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} to {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. There is also a theory that "Okanoue" was originally read "Okanokami," and is a corruption of "Uka no kami." Within the precincts stands a {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} called "Ōkusu of Okanomiya," designated a Tokushima Prefectural Natural Monument. It has long been called the "Sacred Mirror Tree." With a girth of about 25 m, its trunk splits from one root into three, and one of those splits again, so four trunks stand side by side as if pressing against each other. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} === Precinct shrine === * Jisha Shrine === Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Itano Interchange|Q11105197|WD=Q11105197|1=Itano interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Itano Station|Q8190673|WD=Q8190673|1=Itano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kōtoku Line|Q859984|WD=Q859984|1=Kōtoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/tokushima/364045/detail/36404ac2100120888 岡上神社の大クス 日本観光振興協会] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata content == {{神社 |名称= Okanoue Shrine |画像= [[File:Okanoue shrine, Tokushima.jpg|250px]]
Shimenawa pillar and torii |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}} Ōtera Aza Okayamaji 7 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former village shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Okanoue Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okanoue Shrine''' (おかのうえじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its year of foundation is unknown. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}} (Kansei 1), it moved to the current site. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here and prayed for lasting military fortune while heading from landing at Katsuura in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} to {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. There is also a theory that "Okanoue" was originally read "Okanokami," and is a corruption of "Uka no kami." Within the precincts stands a {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} called "Ōkusu of Okanomiya," designated a Tokushima Prefectural Natural Monument. It has long been called the "Sacred Mirror Tree." With a girth of about 25 m, its trunk splits from one root into three, and one of those splits again, so four trunks stand side by side as if pressing against each other. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} === Precinct shrine === * Jisha Shrine === Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Itano Interchange|Q11105197|WD=Q11105197|1=Itano interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Itano Station|Q8190673|WD=Q8190673|1=Itano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kōtoku Line|Q859984|WD=Q859984|1=Kōtoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/tokushima/364045/detail/36404ac2100120888 岡上神社の大クス 日本観光振興協会] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:板野町の建築物]] [[Category:板野町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]]

content == {{神社 |名称= Okanoue Shrine |画像= [[File:Okanoue shrine, Tokushima.jpg|250px]]
Shimenawa pillar and torii |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|WD=Q1156025|1=Itano District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}} Ōtera Aza Okayamaji 7 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former village shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Okanoue Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okanoue Shrine''' (おかのうえじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Itano, Tokushima|Q1350904|WD=Q1350904|1=Itano, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === Its year of foundation is unknown. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}} (Kansei 1), it moved to the current site. It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} worshipped here and prayed for lasting military fortune while heading from landing at Katsuura in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} to {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. There is also a theory that "Okanoue" was originally read "Okanokami," and is a corruption of "Uka no kami." Within the precincts stands a {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} called "Ōkusu of Okanomiya," designated a Tokushima Prefectural Natural Monument. It has long been called the "Sacred Mirror Tree." With a girth of about 25 m, its trunk splits from one root into three, and one of those splits again, so four trunks stand side by side as if pressing against each other. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} === Precinct shrine === * Jisha Shrine === Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Itano Interchange|Q11105197|WD=Q11105197|1=Itano interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Itano Station|Q8190673|WD=Q8190673|1=Itano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kōtoku Line|Q859984|WD=Q859984|1=Kōtoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/tokushima/364045/detail/36404ac2100120888 岡上神社の大クス 日本観光振興協会] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okanoue Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:板野町の建築物]] [[Category:板野町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]]
